Class Game

  • java.lang.Object
    • Game

  • public class Game
extends java.lang.Object
    Represents the state and gameplay of a game of Egyptian Rat Screw.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Game()
      Sets up a new Game with 2 Players.
      Game(int numPlayers)
      Sets up a new Game with the specified number of Players.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      Player getCurPlayer()
      Get the Player whose turn it currently is.
      int getCurPlayerIndex()
      Get the index in the list of Players corresponding to the Player whose turn it is.
      Dealer getDealer()
      Get the Dealer object dealing this Game.
      java.util.LinkedList<Card> getPile()
      Get the Cards currently in the center of the table.
      java.util.LinkedList<Player> getPlayers()
      Get the list of Players in this Game.
      static boolean isMarriage(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
      Checks if there is a marriage on top of the pile.
      static boolean isPair(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
      Checks if there is a pair on top of the pile.
      static boolean isSandwich(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
      Checks if there is a sandwich on top of the pile.
      static boolean isTopBottom(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
      Checks if there is a top-bottom in the pile.
      int play(java.io.PrintWriter outFile)
      Plays through this Game of Egyptian Rat Screw according to the rules until only one Player is left.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Game

        public Game()
        Sets up a new Game with 2 Players. Assigns each Player a pattern to watch for.

      • Game

        public Game(int numPlayers)
        Sets up a new Game with the specified number of Players. Assigns each Player a pattern to watch for.
        Parameters:
        numPlayers - number of Players in this Game

    • Method Detail

      • getPlayers

        public java.util.LinkedList<Player> getPlayers()
        Get the list of Players in this Game.
        Returns:
        the Players

      • getPile

        public java.util.LinkedList<Card> getPile()
        Get the Cards currently in the center of the table.
        Returns:
        the Cards

      • getDealer

        public Dealer getDealer()
        Get the Dealer object dealing this Game.
        Returns:
        the Dealer

      • getCurPlayerIndex

        public int getCurPlayerIndex()
        Get the index in the list of Players corresponding to the Player whose turn it is.
        Returns:
        the Player's position

      • getCurPlayer

        public Player getCurPlayer()
        Get the Player whose turn it currently is.
        Returns:
        the Player

      • isPair

        public static boolean isPair(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
        Checks if there is a pair on top of the pile.
        Parameters:
        pile - the pile of Cards to be checked
        Returns:
        true if there is a pair, false otherwise

      • isSandwich

        public static boolean isSandwich(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
        Checks if there is a sandwich on top of the pile.
        Parameters:
        pile - the pile of Cards to be checked
        Returns:
        true if there is a sandwich, false otherwise

      • isTopBottom

        public static boolean isTopBottom(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
        Checks if there is a top-bottom in the pile.
        Parameters:
        pile - the pile of Cards to be checked
        Returns:
        true if there is a top-bottom, false otherwise

      • isMarriage

        public static boolean isMarriage(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
        Checks if there is a marriage on top of the pile.
        Parameters:
        pile - the pile of Cards to be checked
        Returns:
        true if there is a marriage, false otherwise

      • play

        public int play(java.io.PrintWriter outFile)
        Plays through this Game of Egyptian Rat Screw according to the rules until only one Player is left. Prints the progress of the Game as it plays to a specified output file.
        Parameters:
        outFile - the file to print the Game progress details to
        Returns:
        the position of the winning Player
